name: LIGHTBRIDGE Corp
business: Lightbridge develops next generation nuclear fuel for water-cooled reactors to improve economics and safety of nuclear power plants. It sells nuclear fuel technology to operators of existing and new nuclear reactors, including small modular reactors. The company also targets behind-the-meter customers such as data centers for electric power.
namedSuppliers: Institutul de Cercetari Nucleare Pitesti (RATEN ICN)

exposures.sentence: We are developing next generation nuclear fuel for water-cooled reactors that could significantly improve the economics and safety of existing and new nuclear power plants, large and small, and enhance proliferation resistance of spent nuclear fuel while supplying clean energy to the electric grid or to behind the meter customers for electric power, including data centers.
U.S. and international electricity demand is growing rapidly due to AI-driven data centers, electrification, and industrial development.

catalysts.event: Commencement of irradiation testing of Lightbridge Fuel material coupon samples in ATR at INL
catalysts.sentence: We began irradiation testing of the Lightbridge Fuel material coupon samples in the Advanced Test Reactor (ATR) in November 2025.

catalysts.event: Initial batch of partially irradiated fuel material coupon samples expected out of ATR
catalysts.sentence: We expect the initial batch of partially irradiated fuel material coupon samples to come out of the ATR in 2026, with post-irradiation examination anticipated to begin in late 2026 or early 2027.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 of RATEN ICN feasibility study completed
catalysts.sentence: The Phase 1 work was completed as of December 31, 2025.

catalysts.event: SPPA Modification No. 3 extending umbrella agreement to 12 years
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the Company and BEA entered into Modification No. 3 to the SPPA extending the duration of this umbrella agreement from 7 years to 12 years.

catalysts.event: CRADA Modification No. 1 extending term to September 2032
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the Company and BEA entered into Modification No. 1 to the CRADA, extending the end of the term from September 27, 2029 to September 27, 2032.

catalysts.event: NAS software code development agreement completion window
catalysts.sentence: The work scope includes hold-point reviews and deliverable acceptance by Lightbridge with an estimated completion window between October 2026 through January 2027.

catalysts.event: Studsvik Scandpower agreement development expected completion
catalysts.sentence: The total contract value for development of the code is approximately $0.3 million, payable upon the completion of work, expected in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: Critical Heat Flux Test Program Phase 1 estimated completion
catalysts.sentence: Phase 1 work includes prototype fuel simulator design, fabrication, and acceptance testing and is estimated to take one year to complete and cost approximately $0.5 million.
In January 2025, we signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Oklo, Inc. (Oklo).

catalysts.event: Master Services Agreement with Amentum Technology Inc. signed
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, the Company entered into a Master Services Agreement (MSA) with Amentum Technology Inc. (Amentum) relating to the performance of activities by Amentum in support of the co-location feasibility study under the MOU with Oklo, as well as other activities as the Company and Amentum may agree from time to time.

catalysts.event: MOU and Collaboration Agreement with cladding alloy manufacturer signed
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, Lightbridge entered into an MOU and a separate Collaboration Agreement with a U.S. manufacturer specializing in advanced specialty metals to support the research and development of cladding alloy compositions for potential use as cladding materials for Lightbridge Fuel.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. electricity demand expected to grow by ~3 million gigawatt hours over next 20 years, increasing by ~70%
tamClaims.sentence: U.S. and international electricity demand is growing rapidly due to AI-driven data centers, electrification, and industrial development. Solely in the U.S., demand is expected to grow by ~3 million gigawatt hours over the next 20 years, increasing demand by ~70% from current day.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. Federal Government target to expand nuclear generation capacity by approximately 300 gigawatts electric by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: Interest in advanced nuclear generating technologies is high, punctuated by the U.S. Federal Government s target to expand nuclear generation capacity by approximately 300 gigawatts electric by 2050.

tamClaims.claim: Nuclear energy supplied approximately 9% of global electricity generation in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: According to the IEA, nuclear energy supplied approximately 9% of global electricity generation in 2024, maintaining its role as one of the world s largest sources of low-carbon power.

tamClaims.claim: Global nuclear output reached an estimated 2.7 million gigawatt hours in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: Global nuclear output reached an estimated 2.7 million gigawatt hours in 2024, the highest annual generation on record, according to the WNA.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 438 operable commercial nuclear power reactors worldwide, representing about 397 gigawatts electric of generating capacity
tamClaims.sentence: According to the WNA, as of December 2025, there were approximately 438 operable commercial nuclear power reactors worldwide, representing about 397 gigawatts electric of generating capacity.

tamClaims.claim: 71 reactors under construction globally, with 120 reactors in advanced planning stages
tamClaims.sentence: In addition, 71 reactors were under construction globally, with 120 reactors in advanced planning stages.

tamClaims.claim: IEA projects global nuclear capacity could expand by up to 70% by 2035
tamClaims.sentence: Globally, the IEA projects global nuclear capacity could expand by up to 70% by 2035 and according to the World Economic Forum, there are approximately 70 reactors under construction across 15 countries, with 115 further reactors planned.
